title: Trump gives EU until 4 July to implement trade deal or face 'much higher' tariffs
content: After speaking with Ursula von der Leyen on Thursday evening, Donald Trump delayed his threat of "much higher" tariffs on the European Union until 4 July. Von der Leyen hailed "good progress" towards implementation.

title: EU critic Rumen Radev named new Bulgarian prime minister
content: Radev won the first outright parliamentary majority in Bulgarian elections since 1997 last month on promises to fight corruption.
snippet: Radev won the first outright parliamentary majority in Bulgarian elections since 1997 last month on promises to fight corruption.

title: ‘We will not be bullied’: MEPs dig in over delayed US trade deal
content: Nine months after the EU and US struck a controversial trade deal, negotiations remain deadlocked as MEPs resist pressure to fast-track approval without safeguards against future American threats.
snippet: Nine months after the EU and US struck a controversial trade deal, negotiations remain deadlocked as MEPs resist pressure to fast-track approval without safeguards against future American threats.

title: Europe needs to be more radical on the energy crisis. But how? EU Climate Commissioner explains
content: The only way out of energy crises fueled by the wars in Iran and Ukraine is homegrown energy, EU Climate Commissioner Wopke Hoekstra told Euronews. "We owe it to our citizens and our companies."
snippet: The only way out of energy crises fueled by the wars in Iran and Ukraine is homegrown energy, EU Climate Commissioner Wopke Hoekstra told Euronews. "We owe it to our citizens and our companies."

title: Ukrainian negotiator in US in bid to revive talks with Russia, Zelenskyy says
content: US-mediated talks on ending Europe's worst conflict since World War II have shown little progress since February, when Washington shifted focus to its war with Iran.
snippet: US-mediated talks on ending Europe's worst conflict since World War II have shown little progress since February, when Washington shifted focus to its war with Iran.

title: Commissioner vows to end poverty in 25 years as EU unveils first major strategy
content: “We want to eradicate poverty in the EU in 25 years,” European Commission Vice-President Roxana Mînzatu told Euronews in an interview. However, the anti-poverty strategy lacks a dedicated budget and relies mainly on recommendations rather than binding legislation.

title: Spain ‘more convinced of its own position’ on the US than the EU is, Jaume Duch tells Euronews
content: The Catalan regional minister told Euronews that, while Spain has been leading when it comes to standing up against Washington, the EU has taken a firmer stance in recent months.
snippet: The Catalan regional minister told Euronews that, while Spain has been leading when it comes to standing up against Washington, the EU has taken a firmer stance in recent months.

title: Divisions in Brussels as EU negotiators face crunch talks on US trade deal
content: MEPs and EU countries are facing growing pressure just hours before key talks on implementing the EU–US trade deal. However, officials warn that the Parliament’s demands may be too ambitious to secure an agreement this evening.

title: French carrier group moves south of Suez ahead of conditional UK-French Hormuz mission
content: The carrier’s southward move puts French air assets within range of the strait without entering the Gulf, where the US Navy has been blockading Iranian ports since 13 April.
snippet: The carrier’s southward move puts French air assets within range of the strait without entering the Gulf, where the US Navy has been blockading Iranian ports since 13 April.

title: Portugal arrests further 15 police officers in ongoing Lisbon rape and torture probe
content: According to the Portuguese press, the victims were mainly undocumented foreigners, homeless people or drug users.

title: Son of Myanmar's ousted leader appeals to Macron for help in securing proof of life
content: The military's 2021 takeover prompted widespread public protests, whose violent suppression by security forces triggered an armed resistance that has now led to a state of civil war.
snippet: The military's 2021 takeover prompted widespread public protests, whose violent suppression by security forces triggered an armed resistance that has now led to a state of civil war.

title: EU trade chief urges US to ‘swiftly’ restore 15% tariff arrangement

title: Kyiv slams Egypt for accepting Russian‑shipped grain stolen from Ukraine
content: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said on Tuesday that Egypt has received a fourth shipment of stolen Ukrainian grain via Russia, accusing Cairo of violating its promises.
snippet: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said on Tuesday that Egypt has received a fourth shipment of stolen Ukrainian grain via Russia, accusing Cairo of violating its promises.

title: End of conflict in the Middle East or recession in Europe, IMF warns
content: The IMF’s outlook for Europe remains closely tied to geopolitical developments. A prolonged crisis risks tipping Europe into recession.
snippet: The IMF’s outlook for Europe remains closely tied to geopolitical developments. A prolonged crisis risks tipping Europe into recession.

title: Sweden plans electronic bracelets to monitor children at risk of gang recruitment
content: Criminal gangs in Sweden increasingly recruit children to commit crimes, knowing they will not face prison time if caught as the age of criminal responsibility is 15.

title: EU steps up coordination on hantavirus but insists risks for citizens are 'low'

title: Greek coastguard accused of migrant boat pushback in Aegean Sea, NGO says

title: Can Europe stand up to China on trade?
content: Trade relations between the two economic superpowers have soured in recent weeks as Beijing has attacked EU plans to cut its reliance on China for key goods, services and resources.

title: Brussels mulls scrapping methane fines amid energy crisis - leak

title: Professor expelled from university for inventing 'fake Nobel prize' and awarding it to himself
content: A lecturer-researcher from Besançon is at the centre of a scandal rocking academia: he is suspected of having fabricated an international philology award in order to give it to himself. The ruse has seen him expelled from his university.

title: Iran could strangle Strait in future crisis, ex-Trump envoy warns
content: After more than two months of war between the US and Iran, a deal appears in sight to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's vital shipping routes. However, former Trump official Kurt Volker says the conflict has effectively handed control of the Strait to Tehran.

title: French right-wing broadcaster shares fake magazine cover of French National Assembly president
content: Political commentators mocked Yaël Braun-Pivet, president of France’s National Assembly, for featuring on the cover of tabloid magazine "Closer" on 4 May, except that the image was made by AI.

title: Voting under way in UK local elections seen as a verdict on Keir Starmer's leadership

title: Brussels makes progress but no breakthrough on EU-US trade deal

title: DMA: How the EU’s big tech law changed citizens’ lives
content: Under the DMA, Europeans can choose their default browser, pay with rival apps, and message across platforms. Enforcement remains a work in progress, and many citizens do not know the law exists.
snippet: Under the DMA, Europeans can choose their default browser, pay with rival apps, and message across platforms. Enforcement remains a work in progress, and many citizens do not know the law exists.

title: Meloni -Trump tensions tested, can the US - Italy alliance hold?
